Alex Tianyi Ma

Data Scientist/ML Engineer

  • Projects
  • Blog
  • Contact

I'm Alex Ma

I'm an incoming Applied Math PhD at Denver, CO. I currently help with GenAI model bias research at CU Boulder. I have extensive AI research experiences and skills, complimented by my software and cloud experiences. In my free time I build new data science/ML projects, read books, study audio processing, and play video games.

My research at CU Boulder focuses on discovering and quantifying gender and racial biases in Chinese and English stable diffusion models. I generated facial images using neutral prompts and observed introduced biases.

I have been in the research field since freshmen summer in college. Prof. Lenhart Schubert was kind enough to let me help with his PhD students' research on symbolic Natural Language Processing, consequently taught me computational linguistic, which I took a class about it later.

Because of my NLP background, I was selected to intern at AiiT, Peking University. There, I helped launched a smart customer serivce chatbot and conducted in-depth research on deep learning natural language processing techniques. After the internship, I decided to strengthen my math skills to understand deep learning techniques more deeply. I participated in my awesome math advisor, Prof. Alex Iosevich's summer program on theoretical and computational optimization and data science. My research and experimental experiences taught me to efficiently self study, quickly apply learnt theories, and transform theories into highly performant analysis or models that brings both intellectual and profitable value to the company.

After college, I became a software engineer at Scale AI, a data labeling SaaS startup. There, I learnt valuable data engineering and machine learning skills by implementing, deploying, and maintaining a model that automatically predicts tasker payouts for labeling tasks and replaced manual pricing. Even though I spent less time on creating more innovative solutions, my program contributed to building our platform's data infra, is computaitional efficient, runs end-to-end daily on AWS clouds, and saved laber cost by more than 10%.

I love coding and working with all kinds of data. I also actively interact with peers and mentors in my field, attend events, and enjoy exchanging ideas and experiences. To schedule a chat, feel free to me a message on LinkedIn or email.

Below shows an assorted personal projects I've worked on. It covers areas like data engineering, end-to-end machine learning, deep learning, and statistical and data analysis. Language used range from Python, R, to SQL and JavaScript.
